title: equiv_* doesn't respect $assume
body:
### Version

main

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

Originally analyzed by @KrystalDelusion 
```tcl
read_verilog -sv <<EOT
module gold (input D, output Q);
assign Q = '0;
endmodule

module gate (input D, output Q);
assume property (D == '0);
assign Q = D;
endmodule
EOT

stat gate
chformal -lower
stat gate
async2sync

equiv_make gold gate equiv
#opt_clean -purge equiv
#show equiv
equiv_simple equiv
#equiv_induct equiv
equiv_status -assert equiv
```

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4927c47f-b2de-455b-8bee-860f7a20730b)

### Expected Behavior

Equivalence proven

### Actual Behavior

Equivalence unproven. This is surprising since equiv_* passes use SatGen infrastructure which should construct the corresponding operations for ezsat
code_blocks:
read_verilog -sv <<EOT
module gold (input D, output Q);
assign Q = '0;
endmodule

module gate (input D, output Q);
assume property (D == '0);
assign Q = D;
endmodule
EOT

stat gate
chformal -lower
stat gate
async2sync

equiv_make gold gate equiv
#opt_clean -purge equiv
#show equiv
equiv_simple equiv
#equiv_induct equiv
equiv_status -assert equiv
